title: Niteshift launches with $7M to reduce coding-agent lock-in
friendlyTitle: Niteshift wants to reduce coding-agent lock-in
summary: Datadog veterans launched Niteshift with a $7 million seed round, positioning it as infrastructure for coding agents that can route work across multiple models and support enterprise testing, vetting, and production workflows.
friendlySummary: Datadog veterans launched Niteshift with seed funding to help companies route coding-agent work across multiple models.
whyItMatters: Companies are beginning to treat coding agents like cloud infrastructure: useful, but too strategic to lock to one vendor. Neutral orchestration could matter as model providers move further up the software stack.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Companies may not want important software workflows locked to one AI vendor, just like they avoid risky cloud lock-in.
